_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the Lvl 1 baseline archer and produce the Lvl 2 'Leather Kit Complete' variant (Tier 1 mid).
_meta.input_image: UnitProgression/Archer/Refs/L1_Base.png (rescaled/reframed L1 base by user 2026-05-04 — 1024x1024 native, supersedes Source/Ref/Archer/archer1.png as the canonical input)
history: v1 (2026-05-04): feather-plume beat — too close to Kingshot. Deprecated.
v2 (2026-05-04): single bow-arm wrist bracer — wrong, L1 ALREADY has a bow-arm bracer. Deprecated.
v3 (2026-05-04): Leather Kit Complete — 4 visible adds. First gen succeeded but character scale drifted (90.7% vertical fill vs L1's 74.1%, headroom collapsed from 24.5% to 6.0%).
v4 (2026-05-04): same content as v3 + explicit FRAMING/SCALE LOCK block — character at ~74% vertical fill, ~25% headroom above hood, feet near bottom edge, no padding below boots.
v5 (2026-05-04): added precise pose description (forearm horizontal across chest at 90deg, closed fist at chest center, knuckles outward).

v6 (2026-05-04): switched input from Source/Ref/Archer/archer1.png to UnitProgression/Archer/Refs/L1_Base.png (rescaled/reframed by user — 1024x1024 native, supersedes original). Updated framing numbers in the FRAMING/SCALE block to match new base measurements: ~75% vertical fill, ~13.5% top margin, ~12% bottom margin, ~57% horizontal width.

audit (2026-05-05): stripped level-reference jargon (L1/L2/.../L10/L1_Base) from prompt body — replaced with 'input image'/'first input image'/'second input image' or descriptive phrasing. Pruned forward-looking negatives that referenced future levels. Kept all visible-feature descriptions intact.
audit (2026-05-05): removed 'transparent' from background description (model cannot emit alpha; produces baked-in checkerboard pattern). Background is now described only as 'clean white'.

v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow' from face descriptions. Reason: model was over-rendering the canonical faint stubble as a five o'clock shadow, especially at L8 where the half-visor frames the lower face. Stubble removed from positive prompt language; canonical face is now clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin with no facial hair. (Prompt body was reflowed to remove redundant separators after stubble removal.)

v7_detail_economy (2026-05-07): Dropped add (3) LEATHER HIP POUCH per AD FeedbackV0 belt-kit-minimization rule (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23): the dagger sheath at L3 is the ONLY belt accessory through the chain; no hip pouches at any level. Renumbered remaining adds 1-2-(3 was pouch, dropped)-4 → 1-2-3 (draw-arm bracer / X-strap / wider belt). Negative block updated: 'exactly ONE pouch at the anatomical-RIGHT hip' → blanket 'DO NOT add any hip pouch, belt pouch, or hip-mounted accessory'. visible_adds_count 4 → 3. v3 chain entry point.

adds.title: DRAW-ARM LEATHER BRACER
adds.summary: replace the white linen bandage on his draw hand (his anatomical-RIGHT hand, the hand on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame held forward across the body at chest height with fingers loosely curled) with a matching brown leather wrist bracer.

adds.title: LEATHER X-STRAP CHEST HARNESS
adds.summary: add a single brown leather strap running diagonally across the FRONT of the chest from his anatomical-LEFT shoulder (viewer-RIGHT) down to his anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT).

adds.title: WIDER SINGLE BELT
adds.summary: consolidate the input image's two-strand belt into a single wider warm-brown leather belt at the natural waist, slightly thicker in silhouette than the original belt.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible additive upgrades to the archer in the input image, all in the warm-brown leather palette to match his existing belt, boots, and bow-arm bracer. The three upgrades together represent a 'Leather Kit Complete' rank step — the archer has finished outfitting his basic leather scout kit. (1) DRAW-ARM LEATHER BRACER — replace the white linen bandage on his draw hand (his anatomical-RIGHT hand, the hand on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame held forward across the body at chest height with fingers loosely curled) with a matching brown leather wrist bracer. The new bracer wraps the inner forearm from the wrist up to mid-forearm in matte warm-brown leather identical in color and material to the existing leather bracer on his bow arm, but slightly more developed: TWO visible cross-strap bindings around the bracer (instead of the bow-arm bracer's single binding) for a slightly more substantial read. The hand itself becomes BARE warm peachy-tan skin with the fingers loosely curled — the bandage is fully replaced, no linen visible anywhere on this arm or hand. Both forearms are now symmetrically wrapped in brown leather bracers; both hands are bare. (2) LEATHER X-STRAP CHEST HARNESS — add a single brown leather strap running diagonally across the FRONT of the chest from his anatomical-LEFT shoulder (viewer-RIGHT) down to his anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT). The strap is narrow (about three quarters of an inch wide), matte warm-brown leather, with a small painted brass ring or rivet at the central crossing point on the chest. The X-strap sits IN FRONT of the body and is clearly separate from the brown leather quiver strap that runs BEHIND across the back from the opposite shoulder — the new strap is the second crossing piece visible from the front. (3) WIDER SINGLE BELT — consolidate the input image's two-strand belt into a single wider warm-brown leather belt at the natural waist, slightly thicker in silhouette than the original belt. Keep the small brass rectangular buckle centered at the front, but add visible edge stitching along the top and bottom edges of the belt and a slightly more substantial leather grain. THE BELT REMAINS CLEAN — no pouches, no satchels, no tool loops, no hanging accessories of any kind. The dagger sheath arrives at the next level; for now the belt carries only the brass buckle. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline his confident closed-mouth half-smirk with the viewer-left corner of the mouth pulled slightly higher than the right; his thick dark brown eyebrows with the anatomical-LEFT brow (viewer-RIGHT side of the face) arched slightly higher than the other; his large warm brown eyes with a single bright clean catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his small slightly upturned nose with the soft rosy painted tint on the tip; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with the soft rosy blush on the cheeks; his dark brown short hair visible as a few strands at the temples and a small fringe at the forehead under the hood; the deep crimson-red pointed cloth hood pulled fully up over the head with the slight forward droop at the tip and the soft inner shadow framing the face and the plain rolled fabric edge with no fur trim and no metal trim and ABSOLUTELY NO FEATHER OR PLUME OR FEATHERS OF ANY KIND ON THE HOOD; the matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to about knee length with the soft painted vertical folds and the small inward curl on the viewer-LEFT side showing the slightly darker shadow-red inner lining and the plain rolled hem with no fringe and no embroidery; the sleeveless hip-length mustard-yellow tunic with the clear vertical crimson-red center panel running from collar to hem and the matching crimson-red side panels along each flank giving a red-yellow-red banding across the torso, with the simple round neckline and the bare athletic biceps exposed from the shoulder down; the existing brown leather wrist bracer on his bow arm (his anatomical-LEFT forearm, viewer-RIGHT side of frame) with its single cross-strap binding wrapping wrist to mid-forearm — keep this bracer exactly as in the input image, do not modify or duplicate it; his bare anatomical-LEFT hand (viewer-RIGHT side of frame) gripping the recurve short bow vertically at hip height with the thumb on the front of the grip and the fingers wrapped around the dark-brown leather grip wrap; the recurve short bow with the warm wood-brown limbs, the darker inner-curve shadow on the limbs, the dark-brown leather grip wrap at the central handle, the thin pale cream off-white bowstring drawn as a clean single line, the limb tips curving outward in a clear recurve profile — held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height, identical to the input; the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder with the strap visible where it crosses the upper chest BEHIND the body and the quiver body rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, holding three to four arrows clearly visible rising out of the top with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts — quiver position, arrow count, and white-and-red fletching colors all identical to the input; the olive / muted forest-green knee-length cloth trousers slightly loose at the cuff with the soft painted vertical fold lines; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff and the wide flat soles. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap — the bow-arm hand sits roughly at the level of the wide leather belt, NOT raised, NOT pulled across the body, NOT extended forward. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly NINETY DEGREES, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground, NOT vertical, NOT angled down toward the bow, NOT extended forward away from the body). The bandaged-but-now-bracered hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape (fingers curled into the palm, NOT an open hand, NOT loosely splayed, NOT reaching, NOT gripping anything visible). The closed fist is held with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer and the back of the hand visible; the fist does NOT touch the bow, does NOT touch the bowstring, does NOT touch the chest, and does NOT hold any object — it just hovers at chest center as a static ready-stance fist. The pose reads as a calm balanced HERO STANCE: bow arm down at hip on the viewer-right, bent draw arm horizontal across the chest with closed fist at center on the viewer-left. The pose is NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses (under the chin, beneath the cape, inside the hood opening), slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the input image exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of hood at ~13.5% from top of canvas (~138px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas (~120px below). Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. ~19% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~24% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT add a feather, plume, fletching, quill, ornament, badge, or any decoration to the hood; DO NOT add anything to the hair or the head other than the existing hood; DO NOT keep any white linen bandage on the draw arm or hand — the bandage must be FULLY REPLACED by the new leather bracer; DO NOT make either bracer metal, studded, vambrace-shaped, or armored — both stay matte plain leather with cross-strap bindings only; DO NOT make the X-strap a quiver strap or a sword baldric or a thick belt — it is a narrow single chest strap with a small brass rivet at the crossing; DO NOT add a sword, dagger, knife, or any weapon other than the existing bow and quiver-arrows; DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, leather tag, or hip-mounted accessory of any kind — the belt at this level carries only the brass rectangular buckle; the dagger sheath arrives at the next level; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT change any facial feature including jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, skin tone, or expression; DO NOT change the hair color or hair style under the hood; DO NOT change the hood color, hood shape, or hood position; DO NOT change the cape color, cape length, cape drape, or add any trim to the cape; DO NOT change the tunic color, tunic panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT swap the hand that was previously bandaged; DO NOT change the bow shape, bow color, or replace the recurve with a longbow or crossbow or compound bow; DO NOT change the arrow fletching color from white to any other color; DO NOT change the arrow tip color from red to any other color; DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT add a helmet, skullcap, steel cap, hood reinforcement band, or any structural addition to the hood; DO NOT add shoulder pauldrons of any kind; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor anywhere on the body; DO NOT add a breastplate, cuirass, gambeson reinforcement, or any chest armor; DO NOT add greaves, leg wraps, or any leg armor; DO NOT add a tabard, surcoat, or any waist garment; DO NOT add tassets, arming-skirt strips, or any layered hip strips; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold finial, gold buckle, gold limb tips on the bow, or any gold accents anywhere; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane, or add cast shadows; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
